title: docs: context is not optional for build
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change? Yes

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *vyasgun* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

Sorry, I don't believe this is correct. Context *can be* optional: the `-f` option removes the need.
```console
$ printf "FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018\nRUN date\n" | bin/podman build -f - 
```
That removes the need for a Containerfile, not a context directory.

I'm having a lot of trouble understanding that sentence, so I will focus my comments on the effects of this PR.

This PR removes the brackets around `[CONTEXT]` in the help message and man page synopsis. The effect of removing those brackets is to suggest to readers that `CONTEXT` is a required argument. (I don't care about "context directory". I am talking purely about command-line arguments). This is not an accurate perception, because it is possible to invoke `podman build` with a set of options that eliminate the need for a `CONTEXT` argument. I don't believe this is disputable.

This PR also removes what I believe is a helpful statement, about defaulting to cwd if no `CONTEXT` arg is given.

This is a subtle corner case, and perhaps instead of removing that short paragraph, it could be improved instead?
>When invoked with `-f` and a path to a Containerfile, with no explicit CONTEXT directory, Podman will use the Containerfile's parent directory as its build context.

(This is actually not the same as "current working directory", and I have verified that my assertion above is correct. Podman ignores cwd, it uses the Containerfile parent directory. Trivial to prove.)

And, side note: @vyasgun, THANK YOU for addressing this! My comments are intended as material for reflection, to find a way to more accurately document the behavior of `podman build` in what is actually a pretty common usage. Your PR and initiative are much appreciated.
Agree, I was not familiar with that corner case, and I don't know what happens with it is `-f -` Since there really is no directory for the Contanerfile in this case.  Is it the CWD or /tmp?

@edsantiago Thank you for the review :) I agree, this would have been incorrect without the `-f` being mentioned in the docs and I should have covered it. Should I add back the square brackets around CONTEXT as the newly added description about `-f` flag covers the case when it is optional?
I agree with @edsantiago keep the `[]` brackets to indicate that  it can be optional.  But fix the documentation to say when it is optional
@rhatdan @edsantiago I have made changes as per the comments.

LGTM

My original take on this was that we keep man page in compat with docker https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/build/, i.e make `context` or `file` mandatory so new users who refer to podman's man page have no confusion but after looking at @edsantiago's argument I agree with him that we must document podman's current behavior as is so it does not breaks and old new users both.
